title: QUIC CRT internal early-data prerequisite capture verification
verification_method: Focused requirement-home tests, runtime inspection, and regression evidence.
scope: Verify that the managed client/runtime path captures the positive `max_early_data_size` hint together with the cloned peer transport-parameter snapshot on the correct post-handshake precursor path, that later client setup stores the richer carrier as dormant internal state, that the new material stays absent when the ticket lacks the `early_data` signal, that early data remains explicitly closed, and that accepted resumption completion plus the public surface remain unchanged.
preconditions: The repository builds successfully on net10.0.
REQ-QUIC-CRT-0127, REQ-QUIC-CRT-0128, REQ-QUIC-CRT-0129, REQ-QUIC-CRT-0130, REQ-QUIC-CRT-0131, REQ-QUIC-CRT-0132, REQ-QUIC-CRT-0133, and REQ-QUIC-CRT-0137 remain landed prerequisites.
The diagnostics/qlog family remains separately traced under REQ-QUIC-CRT-0134 through REQ-QUIC-CRT-0136.

supplemental_sections.heading: Acceptance Criteria
supplemental_sections.content:
- The detached carrier captures a positive `max_early_data_size` hint plus the cloned peer transport-parameter snapshot when the correct post-handshake precursor path is present.
- Later managed client setup retains the richer carrier as dormant internal state.
- The new material stays absent when the `early_data` signal is missing, and early data remains explicitly closed before and after capture.
- The accepted abbreviated resumption boundary stays unchanged, and the slice does not claim 0-RTT packet support, anti-replay, key update, or any public API widening.

status_summary: This verification artifact records the internal early-data prerequisite capture slice and points at the requirement-home and runtime evidence that will prove the richer dormant carrier is retained without opening early data or widening the public promise.
expected_result: The managed client/runtime path retains the minimum dormant early-data prerequisite material behind the detached resumption carrier, later client setup stores that carrier as dormant internal state, and the repository does not widen its public promise.
procedure: Inspect the post-handshake ticket parser, TLS bridge state, connection runtime, detached carrier, and client setup seams for a captured positive `max_early_data_size` hint and cloned peer transport-parameter snapshot.
Verify that later client setup stores the richer detached carrier as dormant internal state without opening early data.
Verify that a ticket without the `early_data` signal leaves the new prerequisite material absent while the existing resumption credential carrier still works.
Verify that early data remains explicitly closed and that the accepted abbreviated resumption regression remains unchanged.
Verify that the public surface still does not expose 0-RTT or broader resumption promises.
Run the focused REQ_QUIC_CRT_0139 requirement-home tests, the current CRT/API/INT guard set, the full `REQ_QUIC_CRT_` sweep, and repo-local SpecTrace validation plus render checks.
